about_text.value: A historical Macbeth storyworld grounded in the real kings of Alba: Malcolm II, Duncan I, Macbeth, Gruoch, Lulach, Siward, and Malcolm III. This version keeps the dynastic spine, restores the historical color, and adds a secret super-secret route through the haunted edge of the moor.

characters.name: Macbeth
characters.description: Mormaer of Moray and later king of Alba, a capable ruler whose ambition is tempered by statecraft and the burden of legitimacy.

characters.name: Duncan I
characters.description: Young king of Alba, eager to press north and secure Bernicia, but fatally exposed when the campaign goes wrong.

characters.name: Gruoch
characters.description: Queen of Alba, granddaughter of Kenneth III, and the political center around which claim, kinship, and succession turn.

characters.name: Lulach
characters.description: Gruoch's son and Macbeth's heir, briefly crowned at Scone before Malcolm III ends the line.

characters.name: Siward of Northumbria
characters.description: The towering earl who intervenes on the English side, backing Malcolm and forcing Alba into open war.

characters.name: Malcolm III Canmore
characters.description: Son of Duncan I, exile, claimant, and eventual king whose return shifts Alba toward the world of England and the Normans.

prompt_script.value: Malcolm II dies without a living son, and the crown passes through Bethoc and Crinan to Duncan I. The succession is legal, but not settled.

text_script.value: Honor the Dunkeld line and crown Duncan at once. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The court prefers clarity, but clarity only exposes how young and exposed Duncan's reign will be. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The law looks tidy for a moment, then the north begins to measure how long the new king can hold. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Test Macbeth's Moray claim through Gruoch and the older kinship ties. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Macbeth''s claim moves from rumor to politics, and the old order starts counting Moray as a real center of power. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
What looked like a side branch of the family now feels like the strongest current in the kingdom. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Macbeth''s claim moves from rumor to politics, and the old order starts counting Moray as a real center of power. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Hold the court together until every lord has spoken and every hostage is counted. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Delay may prevent one crisis, but it gives every faction time to sharpen its own story. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
A cautious court is still a divided court; the next move will belong to whoever can gather the broadest oath. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The court gets a king, but not yet a settled peace. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The Dunkeld line holds, but the north keeps its own memory. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
A crowned Duncan makes the succession legible, but legibility is not the same as security. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The new king calms the court and gives Macbeth room to watch the future without haste. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The kingdom accepts the compromise, and the chronicle gains a line it can survive with. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Crown Duncan and stabilize the old house. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The crown now looks like continuity to the north, and that is enough to postpone one civil break. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Moray is not just a region now; it is a theory of how the crown might survive. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The old line gains a usable story, and that story keeps blood off the hall for a little longer. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The Moray claim becomes the language of survival, not merely rebellion. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Let Gruoch and the Moray kin frame the claim as restoration. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Siward and the north are already in the wings, and the court starts counting the cost in advance. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The border will come to matter, whether Duncan is ready or not. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The king hears the warning and has time to choose badly or well. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
If the border is part of the opening scene, then the kingdom has already begun the war it will later name. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.

Bring the English threat into the story before it becomes a war.

The old house of Alpin closes with ceremony that cannot hide its instability. In Alba, blood, fosterage, and marriage now matter more than any single proclamation, and Macbeth is already watching how quickly a crown can become a target.

prompt_script.value: Duncan covets Bernicia and sends his army into Northumbria, laying siege to Durham in 1039. The campaign fails, and the king comes home weakened.

text_script.value: Push the siege harder, even if the host thins at Durham. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The army gains urgency but loses strength, and the failed campaign begins to look like proof that Duncan reached too far. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Northumbria remains outside his grasp, and the king returns with less fear around him than before. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The army gains urgency but loses strength, and the failed campaign begins to look like proof that Duncan reached too far. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Withdraw before the losses become a public humiliation. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Retreat preserves men, but it also advertises caution at the very moment the kingdom wants force. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
A king who turns back from Durham returns home with fewer dead and more questions. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Let the defeat work back into the court and turn the nobles against the king. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The court hears of the failure before it hears the excuses, and the story of Duncan''s weakness spreads quickly. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
What began as foreign policy becomes domestic leverage for the first serious challenger. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The court hears of the failure before it hears the excuses, and the story of Duncan''s weakness spreads quickly. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.

The siege reveals the distance between ambition and capacity. Duncan has the blood of a ruler, but not yet the authority to force the north into obedience, and every lord in Alba can see that Siward is not an enemy to underestimate.

prompt_script.value: The revolt led by Macbeth gathers force after Duncan's losses. Near Elgin, at Pitgaveny, the king is slain and the claim changes hands in blood.

text_script.value: Move openly and seize the throne before Duncan's allies regroup. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The crown now belongs to whoever can occupy it, and Macbeth proves he can move faster than the surviving royal party. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Duncan's death ends the argument for one claimant and begins the harder argument over legitimacy. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Let Gruoch and the Moray kin present the claim as continuity, not usurpation. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Gruoch gives the claim a family face, and the coup begins to look like restoration to those who benefit from it. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The north hears not merely rebellion but a line of descent that can be narrated in public. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Gruoch gives the claim a family face, and the coup begins to look like restoration to those who benefit from it. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Stage the killing as justice for a failed king rather than a naked coup. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
A justified death is still a death, but the framing matters long enough for the new reign to take root. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The court has blood on its hands and a story in its mouth; both will matter. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.

This is the moment the chronicles remember as the hinge between legal kingship and actual power. Duncan's army is broken, the court hesitates, and Macbeth steps into a vacancy that no speech can repair.

encounters.title: Act II: Macbeth Takes the Crown

prompt_script.value: Macbeth reigns from 1040, and the kingdom does not immediately collapse. The Prophecy of Berchan remembers an era of prosperity rather than terror.

text_script.value: Rule as a serious king and make the court obey the roads and the law. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
A steadier reign gives the nobles room to accept Macbeth as more than an interlude. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Prosperity does not erase the coup, but it makes the reign harder to dismiss. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
A steadier reign gives the nobles room to accept Macbeth as more than an interlude. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Bind Gruoch and Lulach more tightly into the succession. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The dynasty becomes visible as a household, not only a battlefield claim, and that steadies the throne. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Succession is politics in Alba, and family is the sharpest language available. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Use the peace to cultivate legitimacy abroad, especially through Rome. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Rome offers not just piety but recognition, and recognition can outlast one battlefield or one sermon. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The pilgrimage makes Macbeth legible to the wider Christian world and harder to caricature at home. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Rome offers not just piety but recognition, and recognition can outlast one battlefield or one sermon. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.

The historical Macbeth is not the stage tyrant. He rules for years, keeps Alba together, and makes a pilgrimage to Rome in 1050, a gesture that suggests both confidence and a desire for recognition beyond the local feud of kings.

prompt_script.value: In 1045 Crinan, Duncan's father, rises against Macbeth. The rebellion fails, and Crinan is killed.

text_script.value: Crush the revolt quickly and make the cost unmistakable. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The rebellion dies fast enough to remind the court that Macbeth is not merely holding the throne; he is willing to defend it. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Crinan''s death turns private grievance into dynastic warning. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Offer terms to the old house and risk looking weak. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Mercy might quiet one feud, but it also tells every claimant that the kingdom can be tested. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
A generous king is only safe if his generosity is read as strength. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Mercy might quiet one feud, but it also tells every claimant that the kingdom can be tested. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Turn the uprising into proof that Macbeth is defending Alba rather than merely taking it. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.

The king''s defenders now have a cleaner story to tell: Macbeth is the ruler who kept Alba intact when the old house fractured. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Even the opposition has to admit that the revolt made the crown look more necessary, not less. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The uprising is personal, dynastic, and short. It proves that the old king's family still has reach, but not enough force to dislodge Macbeth once the new regime has hardened around him.

prompt_script.value: Macbeth goes to Rome in 1050. The journey marks a reign that wants more than survival: it wants standing, memory, and a place in Christendom.

text_script.value: Let Rome validate the reign and strengthen the king at home. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Legitimacy arrives not only through battle but through recognition, and the court feels the difference. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Rome turns Macbeth into a ruler who can speak to the wider Christian order, not just the local host. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Legitimacy arrives not only through battle but through recognition, and the court feels the difference. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Treat the journey as weakness and use the absence to organize rivals. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Absence creates room for rumor, and rumor is always eager to call a reign fragile. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Any rival who moves during the king''s absence is gambling that ceremony is weaker than ambition. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Tie the pilgrimage to Alba''s prosperity so the court reads it as confidence. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
A prosperous kingdom can afford a pilgrimage, and the court understands the message immediately. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The journey becomes proof that Alba is stable enough to send its king away and have him return stronger. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
A prosperous kingdom can afford a pilgrimage, and the court understands the message immediately. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.

The pilgrimage is the quiet center of the storyworld. It places the king inside a larger moral geography, and it complicates any easy tale of barbarism by showing a ruler who can travel, negotiate, and return with authority intact.

encounters.title: Act III: Siward Crosses the Border

prompt_script.value: In 1054 Siward of Northumbria invades Alba in support of Malcolm, the son of Duncan I. English power reenters the story in force.

text_script.value: Meet Siward in the field and force the issue immediately. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
A direct fight promises clarity, but it also gives the English the battle they came for. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Siward''s arrival means the northern war is no longer a private dynastic dispute. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Let Malcolm''s claim ride with the English host and expose the alliance as foreign-backed. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Malcolm''s claim becomes easier to describe and harder to dismiss once English steel is behind it. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The court now has to decide whether the war is about succession or about the shape of Alba itself. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Malcolm''s claim becomes easier to describe and harder to dismiss once English steel is behind it. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Seek to split the invaders by reminding Alba that not every lord welcomes English influence. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Division inside the realm is still useful, but only if the message reaches the men carrying spears. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
A kingdom in crisis can survive many things; a kingdom that forgets its own loyalties is in danger. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.

Siward is the kind of figure the chronicles flatten into size: almost a giant in stature, and large enough politically to shift the balance of war. His intervention turns Macbeth's reign from internal consolidation to border crisis.

prompt_script.value: The Battle of Dunsinane in Perthshire brings wholesale slaughter. Macbeth survives, but his army does not emerge whole.

text_script.value: Hold the hill and force the invaders into a costly assault. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The hill becomes a symbol of stubbornness, but symbols do not stop slaughter. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Dunsinane hardens Macbeth into a survivor even as it breaks the public illusion of invulnerability. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The hill becomes a symbol of stubbornness, but symbols do not stop slaughter. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Retreat and preserve what remains of the host. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Withdrawal saves men, but it also concedes the image of control to the enemy. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The court will remember the retreat almost as clearly as the battle. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Use the chaos to shield Macbeth''s escape toward a second fight. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Escape is not victory, yet it preserves the possibility that Macbeth''s reign has not ended in one day. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The next encounter will decide whether that possibility means anything. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Escape is not victory, yet it preserves the possibility that Macbeth''s reign has not ended in one day. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.

Dunsinane is the image people remember because it makes the scale of the conflict visible. The battle does not simply decide a winner; it reveals how much of Alba has already been consumed by the struggle over who gets to speak for the realm.

prompt_script.value: At Lumphanan, Macbeth is mortally wounded. The reign that had outlived its critics finally begins to fail at the body.

text_script.value: Let Macbeth keep fighting even after the wound becomes fatal. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The king''s endurance cannot reverse the fact of the wound, only delay its meaning. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Macbeth''s body now bears the history the court had tried to negotiate in words. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Protect the succession and think first of Gruoch and Lulach. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
The succession matters now as a live problem, not a theory. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Gruoch and Lulach are no longer claims on paper but the only bridge across the collapse. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The succession matters now as a live problem, not a theory. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Turn the fall into a last assertion of kingship rather than a rout. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
A dying king can still shape the tone of the ending, even if he cannot shape the outcome. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The defeat at Lumphanan carries the dignity of a final refusal. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.

Lumphanan strips away every easy myth of permanence. Macbeth has survived long enough to make his reign matter, but the wound marks the end of one order and the opening for another claimant to speak in the language of restoration.

encounters.title: Act III: Lulach Crowned at Scone

prompt_script.value: After Macbeth dies, Lulach is crowned at Scone in August 1057. It is the first recorded coronation of any King of Scots.

text_script.value: Treat the coronation as the legitimate continuation of Macbeth''s line. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Lulach''s crown gives the defeated line one last public form. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The ceremony matters precisely because it is the first of its kind in the record. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Lulach''s crown gives the defeated line one last public form. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
View Lulach as a stopgap and plan for Malcolm''s return. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
A stopgap can still move history if it buys time for a new claimant to gather force. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Malcolm''s return will test whether the kingdom remembers Scone or only the battlefield. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Use the ceremony at Scone to anchor the kingdom in ritual before war resumes. The court, the road, and the archive all remember it.
Ritual can stabilize a kingdom for a moment, even when armies are already moving again. The next claim will remember it. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
The coronation turns fragile succession into public memory. The court will count the cost later. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.
Ritual can stabilize a kingdom for a moment, even when armies are already moving again. The kingdom keeps the account. In Alba, the record outlives the moment.

Malcolm''s reign points beyond Macbeth''s life and into the larger transformation of Scotland. He is associated with the English camp, with Margaret of Wessex, with Benedictine monks at Dunfermline, and with a kingdom that is increasingly called Scotland rather than Alba.

By the end of the line, the world has shifted from Macbeth''s precarious legitimacy to Malcolm III''s longer reign and the institutions that follow. The House of Dunkeld, Margaret''s influence, and the widening English and Norman presence all leave their mark. The epilogue now contains a secret branch for the more haunted reading of the kingdom.

prompt_script.value: Macbeth''s reign is remembered as legitimate and outward-looking, strengthened by pilgrimage, ceremony, and the patient work of rule.

text_script.value: The kingdom does not escape violence, but it does escape the cheap version of history that reduces Macbeth to a single murderous night. Alba remains a political kingdom with a ruler who understood that legitimacy had to be built in more than one register.

prompt_script.value: Siward''s invasion, Dunsinane, and Lumphanan break Macbeth''s hold on the throne, and the old order burns down around the wound.

text_script.value: The crown passes through smoke and grief, and Lulach''s brief coronation cannot stop the return of Malcolm. The story ends in dynastic loss, but it also records how much history had to happen before Malcolm could inherit it.

prompt_script.value: Malcolm III returns with English support, Margaret of Wessex at his side, and a court that is beginning to look like the future.

text_script.value: Dunfermline, Benedictine influence, new names, and new loyalties all point to a kingdom that is no longer simply Alba of the old chronicles. The house changes, the language changes, and the realm survives by changing with them.

encounters.title: Super Secret Ending: The Inverted Heath

text_script.value: The heath has become a chamber inside the kingdom, and the kingdom a chamber inside the prophecy. Macbeth sees that rule was never only blood and law; it was also the shape of the place that held them. In this inversion, Alba does not simply fall into history. It answers the witches and becomes the riddle that outlives the throne.

prompt_script.value: The witches do not merely predict the crown; they turn the castle and moor into a broken circle where legitimacy folds inward.
